finding: Probability judgments of outcomes increase when the outcome is grouped into a larger category versus smaller category, even when categories are irrelevant and individual outcome probabilities are identical; effect driven by erroneous attribution of category-level likelihood to individual members.

Consumers preserve categories of positive experiences but eliminate categories of negative experiences to manage subjective progression; eliminating a category creates stronger perceived progress than preserving it, explaining differential choices across valence contexts.

Rounded price numbers intensify evaluations when decisions are feeling-driven; nonrounded prices intensify evaluations when decisions are cognition-driven; fit between price form processing fluency and decision context generates intensified product judgments.

Philosophical characterization of imaginative resistance as a phenomenon where audiences struggle to engage with or accept proposed content as fictional; multiple conceptual puzzles arise when authors attempt to prescribe morally or conceptually deviant fictional truths.

Imaginative resistance to morally deviant propositions exists empirically and is modulated by genre conventions and audience genre competence; genre-consistency reduces resistance while genre-violation increases resistance to the same proposition.
